target.title: fix(codex): complete Computer Use tool calls after approval
target.body:
## What this PR fixes

This PR fixes the Codex Computer Use path reported in #96452 in two parts:

1. OpenClaw returns an explicit approval payload for configured Computer Use empty-form approvals:

```json
{ "action": "accept", "content": { "approve": true }, "_meta": null }
```

2. OpenClaw now waits for native tool completion items before firing the completion idle timeout, covering the follow-on `computer-use.get_app_state` timeout reported in review discussion.

The goal is to fix the live failure shape instead of only documenting it.

## Implementation

Approval bridge:

- Adds an optional `emptySchemaAcceptContent` bridge field.
- Sets that field only for configured Computer Use approval elicitations.
- Clones the configured empty-schema accept content per response instead of returning a shared constant object directly.
- Preserves contentless accepts for other empty-schema Codex MCP approval elicitations.
- Preserves deny/cancel behavior unchanged.
- Preserves fail-closed handling for unmappable non-empty schemas.

Native completion handling:

- Tracks active native execution tool items from `item/started` and `item/completed` notifications.
- Blocks completion idle timeout while native execution tool items are still active.
- Releases the completion wait once the active native tool item completes.
- Adds focused turn-watch coverage for waiting on active completion blocker items.

Tests added/updated:

- Computer Use empty-form approvals return `{ "approve": true }`.
- Non-Computer Use empty-form approvals remain contentless.
- Each accepted Computer Use empty-schema response receives a fresh content object.
- Completion idle timeout waits while a native execution item is active.

## Evidence

Current head: `24e340cec50d4fe3a223aa01538920105d7cf03f`.

Repository checks on current head:

- CI/check matrix: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28218810505
- Real behavior proof policy check: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28218809686
- OpenGrep PR diff scan: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28218810513
- Workflow sanity: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28218810470

Local pre-check only:

```bash
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
```

Result: passed before push.

Prior live canary evidence from review discussion:

- Earlier live canary against `c8ee8579b311` timed out after 120s on `computer-use.get_app_state`.
- This PR now treats that timeout as a fix target and includes the native completion wait fix path.

Still needed before merge:

- Current-head routed Computer Use proof showing `computer-use.get_app_state` completes after approval, or a current-head captured failure with narrowed logs if the live path still times out.

## Risk

- Compatibility: approval response payload changes from `null` to an object only for configured Computer Use empty-form approvals. Regression coverage preserves the old shape for other empty-schema approvals.
- Runtime behavior: completion idle timeout now waits for active native execution items. This should prevent premature turn completion while a native MCP tool is still running, but it depends on balanced `item/started` and `item/completed` notifications.
- Security / permissions: this does not broaden Computer Use permissions, bypass OpenClaw approval, or bypass Codex/platform permission checks.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore contentless accepts for Computer Use empty-form approval responses and the prior completion idle behavior. No data migration or irreversible state change is involved.

## Docs updated

- `docs/plugins/codex-computer-use.md` documents the Computer Use-specific approval response boundary and native execution ownership.

## Related

- Issue: #96452
- Review discussion with prior live timeout evidence: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96479#issuecomment-4793142338
- Native completion fix lineage: #96818
target.author: snowzlmbot
